scenes.description: Morning seawall patrol — Oathbrand flares unexpectedly, Pallor tears overhead revealing true starlight, Kaelith chooses silence
Repairing garrison wall — senses surveillance, sees Lirien who doesn't look away, professional predator recognition
Confronts Tavik about the stranger — his transparent lie reveals institutional compromise, Kaelith realizes containment

craftToolsApplied.T5-tone-contract: Maintained morally complex and atmospherically taut tone throughout. Avoided prohibited melodrama and sentimentality. Used dry understated observations (counting stones, 'Fine,' garrison routine).
craftToolsApplied.T6-stakes-escalation: Personal stakes established: Kaelith's containment, surveillance threat, Oathbrand anomaly. Foundation for later escalation.
craftToolsApplied.T7-black-moment-setup: Planted seeds of trust difficulty and institutional betrayal that will pay off in Black Moment.
craftToolsApplied.T8-internal-external-resistance: Internal: guilt-as-identity, self-punishment through exile, distrust of institutions. External: Oathbrand throttling magic, surveillance, Tavik's compromise.
craftToolsApplied.T10-sandersons-laws: Established Oathbrand mechanics (should dissolve but persists), Pallor function (tears revealing structural failure), magic as suppressed capacity.
craftToolsApplied.T12-dual-arc-interleave: Fantasy arc: Pallor tear introduces world-scale threat. Romance arc: Lirien's arrival and predator recognition.
craftToolsApplied.T13-tension-mapping: Chapter sustained tension level 4 (atmospheric, low-grade threat). Ended on hyperalert assessment, priming for escalation.
craftToolsApplied.T15-foreshadowing-ledger: Planted FS-001 (Oathbrand anomaly), FS-002 (Pallor tear), FS-003 (Lirien surveillance), FS-004 (Tavik's lie).
craftToolsApplied.T16-symbolic-motif: Introduced MOT-001 (Pallor as failing membrane), MOT-002 (Oathbrand as externalized guilt). Seawall as structural integrity metaphor.
craftToolsApplied.T17-voice-fingerprinting: Kaelith's voice executed: short declarative sentences, structural metaphors, military precision, emotions as physical sensations, counting as self-regulation.
craftToolsApplied.T18-information-asymmetry: Kaelith doesn't know: who's watching or why, who's maintaining the brand, that she carries Quietus path. Reader knows slightly more.
craftToolsApplied.T20-emotional-throughline: Moved from numb endurance → hypervigilant threat assessment. Body response (shaking hands) showed emotional state without naming it.
craftToolsApplied.T23-mystery-box: Opened MB-001 (Oathbrand persistence), MB-007 (surveillance threat). Deepened both through dramatic showing.
craftToolsApplied.T24-subtext-dialogue: Tavik scene: surface (merchant liaison) vs. reality (institutional compromise). Kaelith reads subtext through his fear.
craftToolsApplied.T25-thematic-scaffolding: TP-1 (trust as structural failure) introduced through Tavik's betrayal and institutional containment. Trust = load-bearing wall metaphor.

foreshadowingPlanted: FS-001: Oathbrand flares when it should have dissolved — someone is maintaining it
FS-002: Pallor tear reveals true starlight — world-scale protective barrier failing
FS-003: Lirien's predator surveillance — professional recognition, kill contract foreshadowed
FS-004: Tavik's transparent lie and fear — institutional compromise, Conclave monitoring
mysteryBoxesOpened: MB-001: Why hasn't the Oathbrand dissolved?
MB-007: Why is Lirien watching Kaelith?
symbolicMotifsIntroduced: MOT-001: Pallor as failing membrane (tear scene, silver knitting closed)
MOT-002: Oathbrand as wound that won't heal (flare, chronic pain, 'should have dissolved')
Seawall as structural integrity metaphor (holds despite decay, like Kaelith's discipline)

emotionalArcHit.start: Numb endurance, exile as penance accepted — counting stones, routine work, suppressed awareness
emotionalArcHit.pivot: Lirien doesn't look away when caught — predator confidence breaks through Kaelith's numbness
emotionalArcHit.end: Hyperalert threat assessment, defensive calculation — shaking hands, resource inventory, containment recognized

continuityEstablished.location: Duskhaven, garrison town, Kaelith's rented room above Listing Mast

knowledgeStates.CHAR-001: Knows: Oathbrand persists abnormally, Pallor tore, someone is watching, Tavik is compromised. Doesn't know: who sent Lirien, why surveillance now, that brand is killing her, that she carries Quietus path.

voiceConsistency.sentenceLengthCheck: 70%+ sentences 3-8 words. Fragments under stress ('Three exits. Stone floor.'). Longer sentences for emotional breach.
voiceConsistency.metaphorCheck: Structural only: load-bearing, mortar, walls hold/crack, settling, keystone. No water/organic imagery.
voiceConsistency.emotionalRegisterCheck: Suppressed-to-physical: brand burns, jaw tightens, hands shake, heat climbs neck. Never named emotion directly.
voiceConsistency.deflectionCheck: Work as deflection (mortar, counting stones), imperatives to dodge emotional territory.

notes: Chapter 1 draft complete. All three scenes executed per outline. Voice fingerprint maintained throughout. Foreshadowing seeds planted for Act I setup. Mystery boxes MB-001 and MB-007 opened dramatically. Emotional arc progresses from resignation to hyperalertness. Atmospheric tone sustained. No info-dumping — worldbuilding woven through observation and physical experience. MRU structure maintained at paragraph level. Chapter ends on defensive calculation and unresolved threat per outline requirements.
